method: Fresh verifier sub-agent and independent adversarial falsifier sub-agent, run in parallel per policy/verification.md §5. Business claim type (>=2 independent Tier<=2 sources required); explicit wire-exclusive check run against the swarm of GlobeNewswire-relay outlets.
origin_items: Surfaced via this cycle's gnews-ai-chips-and-compute feed (101net aggregator, ~2026-07-23T20:00 UTC). dedup-check before opening: clean on the techcrunch.com URL; an earlier check against etched.com produced a moderate/coincidental match to amd-anthropic-compute-deal on generic 'AI chip' title phrasing -- read and dismissed as unrelated (different company, different deal type), consistent with the same false-positive pattern seen on the Amkor story this cycle.

claims.text: Etched closed a $300 million Series C funding round at a $10.3 billion valuation, announced 23 July 2026
claims.reasoning: Confirmed via two independent sources: (1) Etched's own GlobeNewswire press release, and (2) TechCrunch's independently reported story containing an on-record confirmation from co-founder Robert Wachen that the round 'has closed' -- satisfying the >=2 independent sources bar. The swarm of other outlets (Pulse2, Manila Times, 93.3 The Drive, Y100 WNCY, etc.) all verbatim-relay the same press release and were not counted as separate sources (wire-exclusive check applied).

claims.text: The round was led by Sequoia Capital, with Andreessen Horowitz, SK Hynix, Jane Street, and Diffusion Capital also participating
claims.reasoning: Consistent investor list independently reported by both TechCrunch and the company's own press release.

claims.text: Etched was valued at $5 billion in a $500 million round in December 2025, meaning its valuation roughly doubled in about seven months
claims.reasoning: Reported by TechCrunch as established prior context; not disputed by the falsifier.

claims.text: Etched plans to use the funds to scale manufacturing, including facilities in Taiwan and a new site in Milpitas, California
claims.reasoning: Stated in Etched's own press release.

falsifier_notes: Overall verdict: CLEAR TO PUBLISH as confirmed. Falsifier independently found the same $300M/$10.3B figures with no discrepancy across sources, confirmed the round is CLOSED (not merely 'in talks') via TechCrunch's on-record co-founder confirmation, and flagged a critical distinction: a separate, much larger ~$20B Jane Street-led round reported by WSJ in mid-July remains unclosed/in-talks and must NOT be conflated with this $300M round -- the published article explicitly keeps the two separate. mlq.ai appeared in search results (blocked domain) and was excluded by both agents. No contradicting reports found on the core figures.
